title: $subsetreturn
text: $subsetreturn Return only the most recently retrieved occurrences of an entity to its operation entity parameter. $subsetreturn ( Entity ) $subsetreturn ( Entity ) = Expression set | reset $subsetreturn ( Entity ) Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Entity String Entity name. If omitted, the current entity is used. Return Values Table 2. Return Values Data Type Meaning Numeric Number of most recently retrieved occurrences of an entity to its operation entity parameter. If $subsetreturn is not enabled 0 is returned. If an error occurs, $procerror contains a negative value that identifies the exact error. Table 3. Values of $procerror Commonly Returned Following $subsetreturn Value Error constant Meaning -1102 <UPROCERR_ENTITY> The entity name provided is not a valid name or the entity is not painted on the component. -1113 <UPROCERR_PARAMETER> Parameter name not valid or not defined. Use Use in Form, Report, and Service components. Allowed in an operation with an entity parameter, Entity , whose direction is OUT or INOUT. Description An entity parameter usually contains all occurrences of that entity. However, $subsetreturn provides the entity parameter with only the most recently retrieved occurrences of Entity . This is especially useful for, but not limited to, retrieving with retrieve/a . Occurrences that are removed from the hitlist, or deleted from the database are not returned. Example: Return a Hitlist Using $subsetreturn In this example $subsetreturn is used to return a hitlist that includes all customers from the USA: So where's the retrieve profile to get only USA customers? operation GET_USA_CUSTOMERS params entity CUSTOMER : OUT endparams creocc "CUSTOMER", 0 retrieve/a "CUSTOMER" set $subsetreturn end ; GET_USA_CUSTOMERS Copy Related Links retrieve/a Retrieve multiple additional occurrences of the specified entity without discarding the hitlist. set Set the value of the specified ProcScript function to 1 (TRUE). reset Reset the value of the specified ProcScript function to 0.
